assessment.roster_rule: live mode uses current roster/stat/injury APIs only

away.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Jordan Horston
players.injury_comment: Horston exited Friday's loss to the Valkyries in the fourth quarter and did not return, and she'll miss her first game of the season Wednesday. With the 25-year-old wing sidelined, Jade Melbourne, Zia Cooke and Katie Lou Samuelson are candidates for increased playing time.

players.name: Ezi Magbegor
players.injury_comment: Magbegor sustained her injury while playing for Australia in the 2026 FIBA Basketball World Cup Qualifiers, and she appears to be on track to miss the start of the WNBA regular season as a result. The 26-year-old has played a prominent role in Seattle's frontcourt across the past several seasons, most recently averaging 8.0 points, 6.2 rebounds, 2.2 blocks and 2.1 assists in 27.3 minutes per game while starting every regular-season matchup in 2025, while her absence to begin the 2026 season could lead to increased opportunities for Dominique Malonga, Stefanie Dolson and rookie Awa Fam.

players.name: Taina Mair
players.injury_comment: Mair will sit out Wednesday's contest to preserve her eligibility while signed to a developmental pact. Her next chance to play will come Saturday in Phoenix.

injuries.name: Taina Mair
injuries.comment: Mair will sit out Wednesday's contest to preserve her eligibility while signed to a developmental pact. Her next chance to play will come Saturday in Phoenix.

home.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Karlie Samuelson
players.injury_comment: Samuelson is in jeopardy of missing a second consecutive contest due to left adductor tightness. If she's ultimately ruled out, Teja Oblak and Nyadiew Puoch could see increased playing time.

players.name: Holly Winterburn
players.injury_comment: Winterburn is dealing with a right foot sprain, though she was able to play through the injury and log eight minutes off the bench Monday against Minnesota. The guard has seen sporadic playing time this season, averaging 9.4 minutes across seven appearances, so Portland's rotation shouldn't be significantly impacted if she's unavailable Wednesday.
